title: WHOOP Decoded: A Year of Recovery Data

yaml.title: WHOOP Decoded: A Year of Recovery Data

markdown.markdown:
```{r} #| label: setup #| include: false library(tidyverse) library(plotly) library(lubridate) main_df <- readRDS("data/main_df.rds") workouts_clean <- readRDS("data/workouts_clean.rds") n_days <- nrow(main_df) avg_hrv <- round(mean(main_df$hrv, na.rm = TRUE)) avg_recovery <- round(mean(main_df$recovery, na.rm = TRUE)) n_workouts <- nrow(workouts_clean) date_start <- format(min(main_df$date), "%B %Y") date_end <- format(max(main_df$date), "%B %Y") pct_green <- round(mean(main_df$recovery_zone == "Green", na.rm = TRUE) * 100) zone_colors <- c("Red" = "#FF6B6B", "Yellow" = "#FFD166", "Green" = "#00f19f") marker_cols <- zone_colors[as.character(main_df$recovery_zone)] ``` ::: hero ### What does your recovery data actually tell you? ::: stats-row ::: stat-box ::: number `r n_days` ::: ::: label Days Tracked ::: ::: ::: stat-box ::: number `r avg_hrv` ms ::: ::: label Avg HRV ::: ::: ::: stat-box ::: number `r avg_recovery`% ::: ::: label Avg Recovery ::: ::: ::: stat-box ::: number `r pct_green`% ::: ::: label Days in the Green ::: ::: ::: stat-box ::: number `r n_workouts` ::: ::: label Workouts Logged ::: ::: ::: ::: --- :::{.cr-section} ### Chart 1 · Your HRV Over a Year {.cr-narrative} HRV — heart rate variability is time of variation between heartbeats in milliseconds. A high HRV means your body is calm and ready for excercise. A low HRV means your body is still dealing with stress, hard training, or poor sleep. [@cr-hrv] ::: insight **Why it matters:** WHOOP gives you a recovery number, but HRV is the significant figure underneath it. Watching the trend over months reveals your fitness habits. ::: Use the **range slider** at the bottom to zoom into any stretch of weeks. Hover any dot to see the full breakdown for that day. [@cr-hrv] Patterns indicate consistencies or inconsistencies in training and recovery. Dips can be seen after harder training blocks or busier times of the year. Spikes can be seen after rest. [@cr-hrv] :::{#cr-hrv} ```{r} #| echo: false #| warning: false plot_ly( data = main_df, x = ~date, y = ~hrv, type = "scatter", mode = "lines+markers", color = ~recovery_zone, colors = zone_colors, marker = list(size = 6, opacity = 0.85), line = list(width = 1.4), text = ~paste0( "", format(date, "%b %d, %Y"), "
", "HRV: ", hrv, " ms
", "Recovery: ", recovery, "%
", "Resting HR: ", rhr, " bpm
", "Sleep: ", sleep_perf, "%" ), hoverinfo = "text" ) |> layout( paper_bgcolor = "#111111", plot_bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", font = list(color = "#cccccc"), title = list( text = "HRV Over Time — One Dot Per Day", font = list(size = 16, color = "#ffffff") ), xaxis = list( title = "", color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222", rangeslider = list(visible = TRUE, thickness = 0.06, bgcolor = "#1a1a1a") ), yaxis = list( title = "HRV (ms)", color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222" ), legend = list( title = list(text = "Zone", font = list(color = "#888")), font = list(color = "#ccc"), bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", bordercolor = "#333" ), hovermode = "closest" ) ``` ::: ::: --- :::{.cr-section} ### Chart 2 · Which sleep metric actually predicts recovery? {.cr-narrative} WHOOP says sleep drives recovery. But which part of sleep? Total hours? Efficiency? How much deep or REM sleep you got? Use the dropdown to swap the x-axis between different sleep metrics. A tight upward cluster means that metric predicts your recovery. A scattered blob means it doesn't. [@cr-sleep] ::: insight **Common finding:** Sleep performance % and sleep debt tend to be stronger predictors than raw hours — it's not just about quantity. ::: Each dot is one day. The color still shows your recovery zone — green dots should cluster toward the top right. [@cr-sleep] :::{#cr-sleep} ```{r} #| echo: false #| warning: false x_options <- list( "Sleep Performance %" = main_df$sleep_perf, "Sleep Efficiency %" = main_df$sleep_eff, "Hours Asleep" = main_df$asleep_hrs, "Deep Sleep %" = main_df$deep_pct, "REM %" = main_df$rem_pct, "Sleep Debt (hrs)" = main_df$sleep_debt_hrs ) x_names <- names(x_options) sleep_buttons <- lapply(seq_along(x_options), function(i) { list( method = "update", args = list( list(x = list(x_options[[i]])), list(xaxis = list(title = x_names[i], color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222")) ), label = x_names[i] ) }) plot_ly( data = main_df, x = ~sleep_perf, y = ~recovery, type = "scatter", mode = "markers", marker = list( color = marker_cols, size = 9, opacity = 0.78, line = list(width = 0.8, color = "#0d0d0d") ), text = ~paste0( "", format(date, "%b %d, %Y"), "
", "Sleep Perf: ", sleep_perf, "%
", "Recovery: ", recovery, "%
", "Hours Asleep: ", round(asleep_hrs, 1), "
", "Deep Sleep: ", round(deep_pct, 1), "%
", "Sleep Debt: ", round(sleep_debt_hrs, 1), " hrs" ), hoverinfo = "text" ) |> layout( paper_bgcolor = "#111111", plot_bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", font = list(color = "#cccccc"), title = list( text = "Sleep Quality → Next-Day Recovery", font = list(size = 16, color = "#ffffff") ), xaxis = list(title = "Sleep Performance %", color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222"), yaxis = list(title = "Recovery Score (%)", color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222"), updatemenus = list(list( buttons = sleep_buttons, direction = "down", showactive = TRUE, bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", bordercolor = "#333", font = list(color = "#ccc"), x = 0.0, xanchor = "left", y = 1.18, yanchor = "top" )), annotations = list(list( text = "Switch x-axis:", x = 0.0, xref = "paper", y = 1.23, yref = "paper", showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 11, color = "#888") )) ) ``` ::: ::: --- :::{.cr-section} ### Chart 3 · The insight WHOOP never surfaces {.cr-narrative} Here's what WHOOP doesn't tell you directly: **your HRV that morning predicts how hard a workout will feel.** When HRV is low, your heart beats faster to produce the same output — your "easy" run suddenly feels hard. [@cr-workout] ::: insight **What to look for:** A downward trend — higher HRV on the x-axis should mean lower average heart rate on the y-axis. If the pattern holds for you, low-HRV days are days to go easy. ::: Use the **activity buttons** to filter by sport. Running and Jiu Jitsu are the most revealing — cardio sports where heart rate directly measures effort. [@cr-workout] :::{#cr-workout} ```{r} #| echo: false #| warning: false workout_df <- main_df |> filter(!is.na(avg_hr), !is.na(hrv), !is.na(activity_clean)) activities <- sort(unique(workout_df$activity_clean)) n_acts <- length(activities) activity_palette <- c( "Running" = "#00f19f", "Weightlifting" = "#FF6B6B", "Powerlifting" = "#FF9F45", "Jiu Jitsu" = "#A78BFA", "Walking" = "#60A5FA", "Basketball" = "#F472B6", "Dance" = "#FBBF24", "Cycling" = "#34D399", "General Activity" = "#94A3B8", "Other" = "#64748B" ) p_workout <- plot_ly() for (act in activities) { df_act <- workout_df |> filter(activity_clean == act) act_col <- if (act %in% names(activity_palette)) activity_palette[act] else "#94A3B8" p_workout <- p_workout |> add_trace( data = df_act, x = ~hrv, y = ~avg_hr, type = "scatter", mode = "markers", name = act, marker = list(size = 10, opacity = 0.78, color = act_col, line = list(width = 0.5, color = "#0d0d0d")), text = ~paste0( "", format(date, "%b %d, %Y"), "
", "Activity: ", activity_clean, "
", "HRV: ", hrv, " ms
", "Avg HR: ", avg_hr, " bpm
", "Strain: ", round(strain, 1), "
", "Duration: ", round(duration_min), " min" ), hoverinfo = "text" ) } all_btn <- list( method = "restyle", args = list("visible", as.list(rep(TRUE, n_acts))), label = "All" ) activity_btns <- lapply(seq_along(activities), function(i) { vis <- rep(FALSE, n_acts) vis[i] <- TRUE list(method = "restyle", args = list("visible", as.list(vis)), label = activities[i]) }) p_workout |> layout( paper_bgcolor = "#111111", plot_bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", font = list(color = "#cccccc"), title = list( text = "Morning HRV vs. Workout Heart Rate", font = list(size = 16, color = "#ffffff") ), xaxis = list(title = "HRV That Morning (ms)", color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222"), yaxis = list(title = "Avg Workout Heart Rate (bpm)", color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222"), updatemenus = list(list( type = "buttons", buttons = c(list(all_btn), activity_btns), direction = "right", showactive = TRUE, bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", bordercolor = "#333", font = list(color = "#ccc", size = 11), x = 0.0, xanchor = "left", y = 1.14, yanchor = "top" )), legend = list(font = list(color = "#ccc"), bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", bordercolor = "#333"), hovermode = "closest" ) ``` ::: ::: --- :::{.cr-section} ### Chart 4 · Discover Trends {.cr-narrative} Pick any variable for X and Y — every dot is one day, colored by recovery zone. [@cr-explore] :::{#cr-explore} ```{r} #| echo: false #| warning: false explore_vars <- list( "Recovery (%)" = main_df$recovery, "HRV (ms)" = main_df$hrv, "Resting HR (bpm)" = main_df$rhr, "Sleep Performance (%)" = main_df$sleep_perf, "Sleep Efficiency (%)" = main_df$sleep_eff, "Hours Asleep" = main_df$asleep_hrs, "Deep Sleep (%)" = main_df$deep_pct, "REM (%)" = main_df$rem_pct, "Sleep Debt (hrs)" = main_df$sleep_debt_hrs, "Day Strain" = main_df$day_strain, "Resp. Rate (rpm)" = main_df$resp_rate, "Workout Avg HR (bpm)" = main_df$avg_hr ) var_names <- names(explore_vars) make_btns <- function(axis) { lapply(seq_along(explore_vars), function(i) { args <- if (axis == "x") list(list(x = list(explore_vars[[i]])), list(xaxis = list(title = var_names[i], color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222"))) else list(list(y = list(explore_vars[[i]])), list(yaxis = list(title = var_names[i], color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222"))) list(method = "update", args = args, label = var_names[i]) }) } plot_ly( data = main_df, x = ~hrv, y = ~recovery, type = "scatter", mode = "markers", marker = list(color = marker_cols, size = 9, opacity = 0.75, line = list(width = 0.8, color = "#0d0d0d")), text = ~paste0( "", format(date, "%b %d, %Y"), "
", "Recovery: ", recovery, "%
", "HRV: ", hrv, " ms
", "Sleep: ", sleep_perf, "%
", "Day Strain: ", round(day_strain, 1) ), hoverinfo = "text" ) |> layout( paper_bgcolor = "#111111", plot_bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", font = list(color = "#cccccc"), title = list( text = "Explore Your Data — Pick Any X & Y", font = list(size = 16, color = "#ffffff") ), xaxis = list(title = "HRV (ms)", color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222"), yaxis = list(title = "Recovery (%)", color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222"), updatemenus = list( list(buttons = make_btns("x"), direction = "down", showactive = TRUE, bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", bordercolor = "#333", font = list(color = "#ccc"), x = 0.0, xanchor = "left", y = 1.22, yanchor = "top"), list(buttons = make_btns("y"), direction = "down", showactive = TRUE, bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", bordercolor = "#333", font = list(color = "#ccc"), x = 0.45, xanchor = "left", y = 1.22, yanchor = "top") ), annotations = list( list(text = "X axis:", x = 0.0, xref = "paper", y = 1.27, yref = "paper", showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 11, color = "#888")), list(text = "Y axis:", x = 0.45, xref = "paper", y = 1.27, yref = "paper", showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 11, color = "#888")) ) ) ``` ::: ::: --- :::{.cr-section} ### Chart 5 · 365 Days at a Glance {.cr-narrative} Every square is one day. Green means your body was ready. Red means it wasn't. Yellow is in between. [@cr-calendar] ::: insight **What to look for:** Streaks of red often follow heavy training blocks or poor sleep weeks. Green clusters usually mean you backed off at the right time. ::: Scroll back through the year — patterns in the calendar reveal things the line chart buries. A bad month looks very different from a bad week. Hover any square to see the full breakdown for that day. [@cr-calendar] :::{#cr-calendar} ```{r} #| echo: false #| warning: false start_week <- floor_date(min(main_df$date), 'week', week_start = 1) cal_df <- main_df |> mutate( dow = as.integer(wday(date, week_start = 1)) - 1, week_num = as.integer(floor(as.numeric(date - start_week) / 7)), zone_color = zone_colors[as.character(recovery_zone)], hover_text = paste0( "", format(date, "%b %d, %Y"), "
", "Recovery: ", recovery, "%
", "Zone: ", as.character(recovery_zone), "
", "HRV: ", hrv, " ms
", "Resting HR: ", rhr, " bpm" ) ) month_ticks <- cal_df |> mutate(month_label = format(date, "%b '%y")) |> group_by(month_label) |> slice_min(date, n = 1) |> ungroup() plot_ly( data = cal_df, x = ~week_num, y = ~dow, type = "scatter", mode = "markers", marker = list( symbol = "square", size = 16, color = ~zone_color, line = list(width = 2, color = "#0d0d0d") ), text = ~hover_text, hoverinfo = "text" ) |> layout( paper_bgcolor = "#111111", plot_bgcolor = "#111111", font = list(color = "#cccccc"), title = list( text = "Recovery Calendar — One Square Per Day", font = list(size = 16, color = "#ffffff") ), xaxis = list( title = "", tickvals = month_ticks$week_num, ticktext = month_ticks$month_label, color = "#888", showgrid = FALSE, zeroline = FALSE ), yaxis = list( title = "", tickvals = 0:6, ticktext = c("Mon","Tue","Wed","Thu","Fri","Sat","Sun"), color = "#888", showgrid = FALSE, zeroline = FALSE, autorange = "reversed" ), showlegend = FALSE, hovermode = "closest", annotations = list( list(text = "■ Green", x = 0.0, xref = "paper", y = -0.15, yref = "paper", showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 13, color = "#00f19f")), list(text = "■ Yellow", x = 0.15, xref = "paper", y = -0.15, yref = "paper", showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 13, color = "#FFD166")), list(text = "■ Red", x = 0.30, xref = "paper", y = -0.15, yref = "paper", showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 13, color = "#FF6B6B")) ) ) ``` ::: :::

```{r} #| label: setup #| include: false library(tidyverse) library(plotly) library(lubridate) main_df <- readRDS("data/main_df.rds") workouts_clean <- readRDS("data/workouts_clean.rds") n_days <- nrow(main_df) avg_hrv <- round(mean(main_df$hrv, na.rm = TRUE)) avg_recovery <- round(mean(main_df$recovery, na.rm = TRUE)) n_workouts <- nrow(workouts_clean) date_start <- format(min(main_df$date), "%B %Y") date_end <- format(max(main_df$date), "%B %Y") pct_green <- round(mean(main_df$recovery_zone == "Green", na.rm = TRUE) * 100) zone_colors <- c("Red" = "#FF6B6B", "Yellow" = "#FFD166", "Green" = "#00f19f") marker_cols <- zone_colors[as.character(main_df$recovery_zone)] ``` ::: hero # WHOOP Decoded ### What does your recovery data actually tell you? ::: stats-row ::: stat-box ::: number `r n_days` ::: ::: label Days Tracked ::: ::: ::: stat-box ::: number `r avg_hrv` ms ::: ::: label Avg HRV ::: ::: ::: stat-box ::: number `r avg_recovery`% ::: ::: label Avg Recovery ::: ::: ::: stat-box ::: number `r pct_green`% ::: ::: label Days in the Green ::: ::: ::: stat-box ::: number `r n_workouts` ::: ::: label Workouts Logged ::: ::: ::: ::: --- :::{.cr-section} ### Chart 1 · Your HRV Over a Year {.cr-narrative} HRV — heart rate variability is time of variation between heartbeats in milliseconds. A high HRV means your body is calm and ready for excercise. A low HRV means your body is still dealing with stress, hard training, or poor sleep. Watching the trend over months reveals your fitness habits. ::: Use the **range slider** at the bottom to zoom into any stretch of weeks. Hover any dot to see the full breakdown for that day. [@cr-hrv] Patterns indicate consistencies or inconsistencies in training and recovery. Dips can be seen after harder training blocks or busier times of the year. Spikes can be seen after rest. [@cr-hrv] :::{#cr-hrv} ```{r} #| echo: false #| warning: false plot_ly( data = main_df, x = ~date, y = ~hrv, type = "scatter", mode = "lines+markers", color = ~recovery_zone, colors = zone_colors, marker = list(size = 6, opacity = 0.85), line = list(width = 1.4), text = ~paste0( "", format(date, "%b %d, %Y"), "
", "HRV: ", hrv, " ms
", "Recovery: ", recovery, "%
", "Resting HR: ", rhr, " bpm
", "Sleep: ", sleep_perf, "%" ), hoverinfo = "text" ) |> layout( paper_bgcolor = "#111111", plot_bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", font = list(color = "#cccccc"), title = list( text = "HRV Over Time — One Dot Per Day", font = list(size = 16, color = "#ffffff") ), xaxis = list( title = "", color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222", rangeslider = list(visible = TRUE, thickness = 0.06, bgcolor = "#1a1a1a") ), yaxis = list( title = "HRV (ms)", color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222" ), legend = list( title = list(text = "Zone", font = list(color = "#888")), font = list(color = "#ccc"), bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", bordercolor = "#333" ), hovermode = "closest" ) ``` ::: ::: --- :::{.cr-section} ### Chart 2 · Which sleep metric actually predicts recovery? {.cr-narrative} WHOOP says sleep drives recovery. But which part of sleep? Total hours? Efficiency? How much deep or REM sleep you got? Use the dropdown to swap the x-axis between different sleep metrics. [@cr-sleep] ::: insight **Common finding:** Sleep performance % and sleep debt tend to be stronger predictors than raw hours — it's not just about quantity. ::: Each dot is one day. The color still shows your recovery zone — green dots should cluster toward the top right. [@cr-sleep] :::{#cr-sleep} ```{r} #| echo: false #| warning: false x_options <- list( "Sleep Performance %" = main_df$sleep_perf, "Sleep Efficiency %" = main_df$sleep_eff, "Hours Asleep" = main_df$asleep_hrs, "Deep Sleep %" = main_df$deep_pct, "REM %" = main_df$rem_pct, "Sleep Debt (hrs)" = main_df$sleep_debt_hrs ) x_names <- names(x_options) sleep_buttons <- lapply(seq_along(x_options), function(i) { list( method = "update", args = list( list(x = list(x_options[[i]])), list(xaxis = list(title = x_names[i], color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222")) ), label = x_names[i] ) }) plot_ly( data = main_df, x = ~sleep_perf, y = ~recovery, type = "scatter", mode = "markers", marker = list( color = marker_cols, size = 9, opacity = 0.78, line = list(width = 0.8, color = "#0d0d0d") ), text = ~paste0( "", format(date, "%b %d, %Y"), "
", "Sleep Perf: ", sleep_perf, "%
", "Recovery: ", recovery, "%
", "Hours Asleep: ", round(asleep_hrs, 1), "
", "Deep Sleep: ", round(deep_pct, 1), "%
", "Sleep Debt: ", round(sleep_debt_hrs, 1), " hrs" ), hoverinfo = "text" ) |> layout( paper_bgcolor = "#111111", plot_bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", font = list(color = "#cccccc"), title = list( text = "Sleep Quality → Next-Day Recovery", font = list(size = 16, color = "#ffffff") ), xaxis = list(title = "Sleep Performance %", color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222"), yaxis = list(title = "Recovery Score (%)", color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222"), updatemenus = list(list( buttons = sleep_buttons, direction = "down", showactive = TRUE, bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", bordercolor = "#333", font = list(color = "#ccc"), x = 0.0, xanchor = "left", y = 1.18, yanchor = "top" )), annotations = list(list( text = "Switch x-axis:", x = 0.0, xref = "paper", y = 1.23, yref = "paper", showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 11, color = "#888") )) ) ``` ::: ::: --- :::{.cr-section} ### Chart 3 · The insight WHOOP never surfaces {.cr-narrative} Here's what WHOOP doesn't tell you directly: **your HRV that morning predicts how hard a workout will feel.** When HRV is low, your heart beats faster to produce the same output — your "easy" run suddenly feels hard. ", "Activity: ", activity_clean, "
", "HRV: ", hrv, " ms
", "Avg HR: ", avg_hr, " bpm
", "Strain: ", round(strain, 1), "
", "Duration: ", round(duration_min), " min" ), hoverinfo = "text" ) } all_btn <- list( method = "restyle", args = list("visible", as.list(rep(TRUE, n_acts))), label = "All" ) activity_btns <- lapply(seq_along(activities), function(i) { vis <- rep(FALSE, n_acts) vis[i] <- TRUE list(method = "restyle", args = list("visible", as.list(vis)), label = activities[i]) }) p_workout |> layout( paper_bgcolor = "#111111", plot_bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", font = list(color = "#cccccc"), title = list( text = "Morning HRV vs. Workout Heart Rate", font = list(size = 16, color = "#ffffff") ), xaxis = list(title = "HRV That Morning (ms)", color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222"), yaxis = list(title = "Avg Workout Heart Rate (bpm)", color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222"), updatemenus = list(list( type = "buttons", buttons = c(list(all_btn), activity_btns), direction = "right", showactive = TRUE, bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", bordercolor = "#333", font = list(color = "#ccc", size = 11), x = 0.0, xanchor = "left", y = 1.14, yanchor = "top" )), legend = list(font = list(color = "#ccc"), bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", bordercolor = "#333"), hovermode = "closest" ) ``` ::: ::: --- :::{.cr-section} ### Chart 4 · Discover Trends {.cr-narrative} Pick any variable for X and Y — every dot is one day, colored by recovery zone. [@cr-explore] :::{#cr-explore} ```{r} #| echo: false #| warning: false explore_vars <- list( "Recovery (%)" = main_df$recovery, "HRV (ms)" = main_df$hrv, "Resting HR (bpm)" = main_df$rhr, "Sleep Performance (%)" = main_df$sleep_perf, "Sleep Efficiency (%)" = main_df$sleep_eff, "Hours Asleep" = main_df$asleep_hrs, "Deep Sleep (%)" = main_df$deep_pct, "REM (%)" = main_df$rem_pct, "Sleep Debt (hrs)" = main_df$sleep_debt_hrs, "Day Strain" = main_df$day_strain, "Resp. Rate (rpm)" = main_df$resp_rate, "Workout Avg HR (bpm)" = main_df$avg_hr ) var_names <- names(explore_vars) make_btns <- function(axis) { lapply(seq_along(explore_vars), function(i) { args <- if (axis == "x") list(list(x = list(explore_vars[[i]])), list(xaxis = list(title = var_names[i], color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222"))) else list(list(y = list(explore_vars[[i]])), list(yaxis = list(title = var_names[i], color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222"))) list(method = "update", args = args, label = var_names[i]) }) } plot_ly( data = main_df, x = ~hrv, y = ~recovery, type = "scatter", mode = "markers", marker = list(color = marker_cols, size = 9, opacity = 0.75, line = list(width = 0.8, color = "#0d0d0d")), text = ~paste0( "", format(date, "%b %d, %Y"), "
", "Recovery: ", recovery, "%
", "HRV: ", hrv, " ms
", "Sleep: ", sleep_perf, "%
", "Day Strain: ", round(day_strain, 1) ), hoverinfo = "text" ) |> layout( paper_bgcolor = "#111111", plot_bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", font = list(color = "#cccccc"), title = list( text = "Explore Your Data — Pick Any X & Y", font = list(size = 16, color = "#ffffff") ), xaxis = list(title = "HRV (ms)", color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222"), yaxis = list(title = "Recovery (%)", color = "#888", gridcolor = "#222"), updatemenus = list( list(buttons = make_btns("x"), direction = "down", showactive = TRUE, bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", bordercolor = "#333", font = list(color = "#ccc"), x = 0.0, xanchor = "left", y = 1.22, yanchor = "top"), list(buttons = make_btns("y"), direction = "down", showactive = TRUE, bgcolor = "#1a1a1a", bordercolor = "#333", font = list(color = "#ccc"), x = 0.45, xanchor = "left", y = 1.22, yanchor = "top") ), annotations = list( list(text = "X axis:", x = 0.0, xref = "paper", y = 1.27, yref = "paper", showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 11, color = "#888")), list(text = "Y axis:", x = 0.45, xref = "paper", y = 1.27, yref = "paper", showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 11, color = "#888")) ) ) ``` ::: ::: --- :::{.cr-section} ### Chart 5 · 365 Days at a Glance {.cr-narrative} Every square is one day. Green means your body was ready. Red means it wasn't. Yellow is in between. [@cr-calendar] ::: insight **What to look for:** Streaks of red often follow heavy training blocks or poor sleep weeks. Green clusters usually mean you backed off at the right time. ::: Scroll back through the year — patterns in the calendar reveal things the line chart buries. A bad month looks very different from a bad week. Hover any square to see the full breakdown for that day. [@cr-calendar] :::{#cr-calendar} ```{r} #| echo: false #| warning: false start_week <- floor_date(min(main_df$date), 'week', week_start = 1) cal_df <- main_df |> mutate( dow = as.integer(wday(date, week_start = 1)) - 1, week_num = as.integer(floor(as.numeric(date - start_week) / 7)), zone_color = zone_colors[as.character(recovery_zone)], hover_text = paste0( "", format(date, "%b %d, %Y"), "
", "Recovery: ", recovery, "%
", "Zone: ", as.character(recovery_zone), "
", "HRV: ", hrv, " ms
", "Resting HR: ", rhr, " bpm" ) ) month_ticks <- cal_df |> mutate(month_label = format(date, "%b '%y")) |> group_by(month_label) |> slice_min(date, n = 1) |> ungroup() plot_ly( data = cal_df, x = ~week_num, y = ~dow, type = "scatter", mode = "markers", marker = list( symbol = "square", size = 16, color = ~zone_color, line = list(width = 2, color = "#0d0d0d") ), text = ~hover_text, hoverinfo = "text" ) |> layout( paper_bgcolor = "#111111", plot_bgcolor = "#111111", font = list(color = "#cccccc"), title = list( text = "Recovery Calendar — One Square Per Day", font = list(size = 16, color = "#ffffff") ), xaxis = list( title = "", tickvals = month_ticks$week_num, ticktext = month_ticks$month_label, color = "#888", showgrid = FALSE, zeroline = FALSE ), yaxis = list( title = "", tickvals = 0:6, ticktext = c("Mon","Tue","Wed","Thu","Fri","Sat","Sun"), color = "#888", showgrid = FALSE, zeroline = FALSE, autorange = "reversed" ), showlegend = FALSE, hovermode = "closest", annotations = list( list(text = "■ Green", x = 0.0, xref = "paper", y = -0.15, yref = "paper", showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 13, color = "#00f19f")), list(text = "■ Yellow", x = 0.15, xref = "paper", y = -0.15, yref = "paper", showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 13, color = "#FFD166")), list(text = "■ Red", x = 0.30, xref = "paper", y = -0.15, yref = "paper", showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 13, color = "#FF6B6B")) ) ) ``` ::: :::
